title: African Union Junior Program Officer (AfCFTA) Recruitment 2026 in Ghana | Earn Up to US$77,425 Per Year
summary: The African Union (AU) has officially opened applications for the Junior Program Officer (AfCFTA) position at the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) Secretariat in Accra, Ghana. This exciting international career opportunity is ideal for young professionals seeking to contribute to Africa s economic integration while building a rewarding career within one of the continent s most respected intergovernmental organizations. The position offers competitive remuneration, career development opportunities, exposure to high-level regional programmes, and the chance to work directly on initiatives supporting Africa s largest free trade agreement. If you possess experience in monitoring and evaluation, programme coordination, economics, statistics, development studies, public finance, or related disciplines, this could be your opportunity to join the African Union and contribute to the successful implementation of Agenda 2063. About the African Union The African Union (AU) is Africa s premier continental organization dedicated to promoting unity, peace, sustainable development, economic integration, and prosperity among its member states. Through Agenda 2063, the AU seeks to create: An integrated Africa A prosperous Africa A peaceful Africa A citizen-driven Africa A globally influential Africa The African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) is one of the flagship projects under Agenda 2063 and represents one of the world s largest free trade areas. Position Overview Position: Junior Program Officer (AfCFTA) Requisition ID: 3020 Location: Accra, Ghana Department: Director Coordination Programmes Reports To: Program Officer Job Grade: P1 Contract Type: Fixed-Term The successful candidate will support programme coordination, implementation, monitoring, and resource mobilization activities that strengthen funding coordination and ensure effective implementation of AfCFTA programmes. Purpose of the Position The Junior Program Officer will assist in coordinating and monitoring resource mobilization initiatives that support programme implementation across the AfCFTA Secretariat. The role focuses on ensuring: Better coordination among programmes. Effective implementation of funding plans. Improved partnerships. Enhanced monitoring. Reduced duplication of efforts. Sustainable programme financing. Key Responsibilities The successful candidate will perform a wide range of responsibilities, including: Programme Coordination Monitor implementation of resource mobilization work plans. Track programme progress and prepare reports. Support implementation of funding strategies. Coordinate programme activities across departments. Resource Mobilization Review existing resource mobilization frameworks. Support continuous improvement of funding strategies. Assist departments in updating resource mobilization plans. Facilitate financing discussions with stakeholders. Monitoring and Evaluation Work closely with Monitoring and Evaluation teams. Ensure implementation follows established standards. Support performance tracking. Promote accountability throughout programme implementation. Coordination and Partnerships Promote information sharing across departments. Identify and reduce duplication of efforts. Strengthen collaboration among programme teams. Maintain productive working relationships with stakeholders. Capacity Building Support capacity development for AU Member States. Assist in organizing structured financing dialogues. Strengthen institutional knowledge on resource mobilization. Additional Duties The officer may also perform other responsibilities assigned by supervisors or the Director. Educational Requirements Applicants must possess a university degree in one of the following fields: Monitoring and Evaluation Economics Statistics Development Studies Demography Public Finance Or another related discipline Professional Experience Candidates must have: At least two (2) years of relevant professional experience. Experience in programme management, monitoring, evaluation, donor coordination, development finance, or public sector programmes will be advantageous. Required Skills Applicants should demonstrate: Excellent written communication skills. Strong verbal communication abilities. Sound planning and organizational skills. Ability to manage uncertainty. Cultural sensitivity. Ability to work across departments and countries. Strong teamwork skills. Good analytical abilities. Leadership Competencies Successful applicants should demonstrate: Strategic insight Developing others Change management Risk management Core Competencies The African Union values candidates who exhibit: Teamwork and collaboration Accountability Compliance with organizational rules Continuous learning Professional ethics Functional Competencies Candidates should possess: Strong influencing and communication skills. Technical job knowledge. Information sharing abilities. Results-oriented mindset. Continuous improvement focus. Contract Duration The appointment will initially be offered on a fixed-term contract of one or two years. The first six months will serve as a probation period. Following successful performance evaluation and subject to funding availability, the contract may be renewed up to two additional times. Salary and Benefits The African Union offers an attractive remuneration package. Annual salary includes: US$77,425.50 for internationally recruited staff. US$59,805.19 for locally recruited staff. These amounts are inclusive of applicable allowances. Language Requirements Applicants must be proficient in at least one of the African Union s official working languages: Arabic English French Kiswahili Portuguese Spanish Knowledge of an additional AU language is considered an added advantage. Equal Opportunity Employer The African Union is committed to equal employment opportunities. Qualified women are strongly encouraged to apply. The AU also encourages applications from underrepresented member states to promote balanced continental representation. Required Application Documents Applicants should prepare: A fully completed online application. The mandatory African Union CV template. An African passport. Academic certificates and qualifications. Relevant professional certificates. Failure to use the mandatory AU CV template may affect the application. How to Apply Interested candidates should complete the online application through the African Union Careers Portal. Before applying: Download the official AU CV template. Complete the template accurately. Upload all required supporting documents. Submit the application before the deadline. Application Deadline Applications must be submitted no later than 7 July 2026 at 11:59 p.m. UTC . Late submissions will not be considered. Apply Here Why You Should Apply This opportunity provides professionals with the chance to: Work with Africa s leading continental organization. Contribute to implementation of the AfCFTA. Build international development experience. Earn a competitive salary. Develop expertise in programme coordination and resource mobilization. Collaborate with governments, development partners, and regional institutions. Advance a meaningful career supporting Africa s economic transformation. For more information, visit the official website Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post African Union Junior Program Officer (AfCFTA) Recruitment 2026 in Ghana | Earn Up to US$77,425 Per Year appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Nexa Climate and Health Innovation Funding 2026: Up to Transform Local Health Systems Across Africa, Latin America, and the Caribbean
summary: Climate change is no longer only an environmental issue—it has become one of the greatest public health challenges of our time. Rising temperatures, changing rainfall patterns, poor air quality, and the rapid spread of mosquito-borne diseases are placing unprecedented pressure on healthcare systems, particularly in low- and middle-income countries. To help address these growing challenges, Nexa , a global climate and health innovation initiative, is investing in bold, locally led innovations that enable health systems to anticipate climate-related risks and respond before they become health emergencies. Led by Grand Challenges Canada and the Science for Africa Foundation , together with an international consortium of funding partners, Nexa is now accepting applications for its 2026 Climate and Health Innovation Funding Opportunity . Organizations and innovators from Africa, Latin America, and the Caribbean are invited to submit innovative solutions that strengthen healthcare systems and protect vulnerable communities from climate-driven health threats. Application Deadline: July 22, 2026, at 2:00 p.m. Eastern Time (ET) / 6:00 p.m. UTC About Nexa Nexa is a global initiative dedicated to accelerating climate and health innovations that improve the resilience of health systems facing increasing climate-related challenges. Rather than applying one-size-fits-all approaches, Nexa prioritizes locally led innovations , recognizing that health workers, researchers, innovators, and humanitarian organizations working directly within affected communities understand their challenges better than anyone else. The initiative focuses on turning climate-risk information into practical health actions that reduce illness and save lives. Nexa is co-led by: Grand Challenges Canada Science for Africa Foundation Working alongside global partners including: Novo Nordisk Foundation Sanofi Foundation Fund for Innovation in Development Global Grand Challenges Network Botswana Innovation Fund NCST Malawi NCST Rwanda Institut Pasteur Dakar South African Medical Research Council Government of Canada Why Climate and Health Innovation Matters Climate change is creating new health emergencies across the world. Approximately 3.6 billion people currently live in regions that are highly vulnerable to climate change. The effects are becoming increasingly severe, including: Expansion of mosquito-borne diseases into new regions. Increased frequency of extreme heat events. Poor air quality affecting millions of people. Greater pressure on already overstretched health systems. Communities that experience the greatest risks include: Pregnant women Children Older adults People living with chronic illnesses Rural and underserved populations Communities with limited healthcare access Without stronger healthcare systems capable of anticipating these risks, millions of people remain vulnerable. Community-Led Innovation Drives Nexa Unlike many global funding initiatives, Nexa s priorities have been shaped directly by frontline professionals. In January 2025, Grand Challenges Canada, the Science for Africa Foundation, the Global Grand Challenges Network, and The Geneva Learning Foundation launched the largest global climate and health survey ever conducted. The survey gathered responses from: More than 6,400 health and humanitarian workers Across 107 low- and middle-income countries These insights helped identify the most urgent climate-health challenges and informed Nexa s investment priorities. Nexa s Areas of Focus Applicants should develop innovations that address one or more of the following priority areas. 1. Climate-Informed Early Warning and Monitoring Systems Nexa seeks innovations that help communities detect climate-related health risks before they escalate into emergencies. Examples include: Early warning systems Climate surveillance platforms Disease forecasting tools Integrated weather and health monitoring systems Risk prediction technologies Community alert systems Solutions should help communities prepare for: Mosquito-borne diseases Extreme heat Air pollution Other climate-driven health risks 2. Climate-Responsive Patient Care Delivery Healthcare providers need better tools to continue delivering quality care during climate emergencies. Nexa supports innovations that improve: Diagnosis Treatment Patient monitoring Continuity of healthcare services Health service delivery during climate stress Projects may focus on: Heat-related illnesses Air pollution-related diseases Malaria Dengue Other mosquito-borne infections What Kind of Innovations Does Nexa Support? Successful proposals may include innovations that: Combine weather data with health information. Improve disease prediction. Support healthcare decision-making. Enable rapid response before disease outbreaks occur. Improve patient care during climate events. Help healthcare systems remain operational during climate stress. Strengthen local healthcare capacity. Reduce climate-related illness and deaths. Nexa particularly encourages practical, scalable, and community-driven solutions. Why This Funding Opportunity Is Important Climate change affects countries differently. Local innovators understand local realities better than external organizations. Nexa therefore prioritizes: Local leadership Community ownership Evidence-based innovation Sustainable health solutions Scalable technologies Strong partnerships The initiative seeks solutions that can create lasting improvements while protecting the most vulnerable populations. Who Should Apply? This funding opportunity is ideal for: Health innovators Universities Research institutions Technology developers Public health organizations NGOs Healthcare providers Social enterprises Climate innovators Humanitarian organizations Applicants should have innovative ideas capable of strengthening healthcare delivery in climate-vulnerable settings. Benefits of the Nexa Climate and Health Innovation Funding Selected innovators will benefit from: Funding to advance innovative climate-health solutions. Opportunities to scale impactful innovations. Global visibility. Collaboration with leading international partners. Access to an international climate-health innovation network. Support for strengthening local healthcare systems. Contribution to improving health outcomes in climate-vulnerable communities. Important Deadline Applications must be submitted by July 22, 2026, at 2:00 p.m. ET (6:00 p.m. UTC). Late applications may not be considered. How to Apply Interested applicants should review the complete funding guidelines and application requirements before submitting their proposals. Applicants are encouraged to prepare early and ensure their projects clearly demonstrate how they will strengthen climate resilience within healthcare systems. Apply Here Final Thoughts Climate change is rapidly transforming global health challenges, demanding innovative and locally driven responses. Through Nexa, innovators across Africa, Latin America, and the Caribbean have an opportunity to develop groundbreaking solutions that help healthcare systems anticipate climate risks, protect vulnerable communities, and improve health outcomes for millions of people. Organizations with bold ideas that combine climate science, healthcare, technology, and community engagement are strongly encouraged to apply before the application deadline. For more information, visit the official website Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post Nexa Climate and Health Innovation Funding 2026: Up to Transform Local Health Systems Across Africa, Latin America, and the Caribbean appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: MFWA 2026 Fully Funded Training Workshop for Journalists in Anglophone West Africa: Apply to Strengthen Your Reporting on Africa–China Trade, Investment and Political Relations
summary: The Media Foundation for West Africa (MFWA) , in partnership with the Wits Centre for Journalism (WCJ) in South Africa, has officially opened applications for its 2026 Training Workshop on Critical Coverage of Africa–China Trade, Investment and Political Relations . This fully funded capacity-building programme is designed for journalists from selected Anglophone West African countries who are interested in strengthening their reporting on one of the most important global relationships shaping Africa s future. Scheduled to take place in Accra, Ghana , during the last quarter of 2026, the three-day workshop will bring together journalists from across the region to deepen their understanding of the complex political, economic, and social dimensions of Africa–China relations. The organisers aim to equip media professionals with the knowledge, analytical skills, and investigative techniques required to produce balanced, evidence-based, and impactful journalism that serves the public interest. Application Deadline: July 30, 2026 About the MFWA Training Workshop Africa s relationship with China has expanded significantly over the past two decades. China has become one of Africa s largest trading partners and investors, financing major infrastructure projects, supporting industrial development, and increasing cooperation in technology, mining, agriculture, energy, and manufacturing. However, this growing relationship also raises important questions surrounding: Trade and economic partnerships Infrastructure development Development financing Natural resource extraction Labour practices Technology transfer Environmental sustainability Governance and accountability International diplomacy Public policy As geopolitical dynamics continue to evolve, journalists play a critical role in helping citizens understand the opportunities, challenges, and implications of Africa–China cooperation. Reporting on these issues requires more than simply covering official announcements—it demands context, critical analysis, investigative skills, and an understanding of international affairs. Recognising this need, the Media Foundation for West Africa has partnered with the Wits Centre for Journalism to organise this specialised workshop titled Building Capacity to Cover a Complex Story – Africa and China Relations. The programme is funded by the University of the Witwatersrand and is intended to strengthen the reporting capacity of journalists across Anglophone West Africa. What Participants Will Learn The workshop combines subject-matter expertise with practical journalism skills. Participants will gain insights into: Understanding the evolving Africa–China relationship Reporting on international trade and investment Analysing development financing and infrastructure projects Investigating governance and accountability issues Covering natural resource extraction responsibly Reporting on labour, technology, and environmental concerns Applying investigative journalism techniques Producing balanced, accurate, and evidence-based stories Framing stories from African perspectives Understanding global geopolitical shifts and their implications for African countries The training seeks to improve both the depth and quality of reporting on international affairs while encouraging journalism that promotes transparency and informed public debate. Benefits of the Programme Successful applicants will receive comprehensive financial support throughout the programme. The organisers will cover: Return economy-class airfare Accommodation Meals Local transportation within Ghana This ensures participants can focus entirely on learning and networking without worrying about travel expenses. Additional Opportunities After the Training One of the most exciting aspects of this programme is that participation does not end after the workshop. Graduates will have the opportunity to apply for: Reporting grants to produce in-depth investigative stories on Africa–China relations. Funding support for publishing high-quality public-interest journalism. Professional mentorship and continued engagement with experts. Furthermore, three outstanding participants will be selected for a fully funded opportunity to attend the Africa Investigative Journalism Conference (AIJC) , scheduled for 10–12 November 2026 in Kenya , where they will present and discuss their reporting projects. Who Can Apply? Applicants must meet all of the following requirements: Be based in one of the eligible Anglophone West African countries. Work in print, radio, television, or digital journalism. Report on one or more of the following beats: Politics Economy Business Trade Development Environment Extractive industries Infrastructure International affairs Be available during September–October 2026. Obtain a formal permission letter from their editor allowing participation. Be capable of producing an in-depth story after completing the workshop. Why Journalists Should Apply Today s journalists increasingly cover stories that extend beyond national borders. International trade agreements, foreign investments, infrastructure financing, and geopolitical developments directly affect local communities and national development. This workshop provides a unique opportunity to strengthen professional skills while learning from leading experts in journalism and international affairs. Participants will also build valuable regional networks with fellow journalists, exchange experiences, and gain practical tools for producing investigative stories that inform public debate and promote accountability. For journalists interested in politics, economics, business reporting, development journalism, or investigative reporting, this programme represents an important opportunity for career development and professional growth. How to Apply Interested journalists should complete the official online application form provided by the Media Foundation for West Africa. Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted. Applicants are encouraged to prepare their applications carefully and ensure they meet every eligibility requirement before submitting. Application Deadline: July 30, 2026. For more information and to submit your application, visit the official MFWA application page: Discover more global workshop opportunities on OFY here The post MFWA 2026 Fully Funded Training Workshop for Journalists in Anglophone West Africa: Apply to Strengthen Your Reporting on Africa–China Trade, Investment and Political Relations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Gustave Roussy Prize 2027: €200,000 International Cancer Research Award Now Accepting Applications Worldwide
summary: Researchers making groundbreaking contributions to the fight against cancer now have the opportunity to gain one of the world s most prestigious recognitions in oncology. The Gustave Roussy Prize 2027 has officially opened applications, inviting outstanding cancer researchers from across the globe to compete for an award valued at €200,000 . Established by the Fondation Gustave Roussy , with the support of Laboratoires Pierre Fabre as its founding sponsor, the prize recognizes researchers whose scientific discoveries have significantly transformed cancer diagnosis, treatment, prevention, or patient care. Since its launch in 2025, the award has rapidly become one of the most highly respected international distinctions in oncology research, celebrating scientific excellence and innovations that deliver measurable and lasting benefits for cancer patients worldwide. Application Deadline: August 26, 2026, at 4:00 p.m. CEST About the Gustave Roussy Prize Cancer remains one of the world s leading health challenges, affecting millions of people every year. Continuous scientific research has resulted in remarkable progress in early diagnosis, precision medicine, immunotherapy, targeted therapies, prevention strategies, and patient-centered care. The Gustave Roussy Prize was created to recognize researchers whose discoveries have contributed substantially to these advances. The award was launched in 2025 to commemorate two important milestones: The 150th anniversary of Professor Gustave Roussy s birth. The centenary of the Gustave Roussy Institute. Professor Gustave Roussy is widely recognized as a pioneer in cancer care. In 1921, he established one of the world s first medical consultations dedicated specifically to patients with tumors, laying the foundation for modern comprehensive oncology care. Today, Gustave Roussy is recognized as Europe s leading cancer center and continues to drive innovation through cutting-edge research, education, and patient care. Prize Value The Gustave Roussy Prize carries a total value of €200,000 , making it one of the largest international awards dedicated to cancer research. The funding is divided into two components: €100,000 awarded directly to the laureate for unrestricted personal use. €100,000 allocated to support an innovative oncology research project, enabling continued scientific discovery and future breakthroughs. Purpose of the Award The prize celebrates scientific breakthroughs that have created tangible improvements in cancer care and patient outcomes. Research achievements may relate to: Cancer diagnosis Cancer treatment Cancer prevention Clinical oncology Translational medicine Precision medicine Immunotherapy Biomarker discovery Screening technologies Therapeutic innovation Improved quality of patient care Better understanding of cancer biology The emphasis is not simply on academic achievement but on research that demonstrates measurable clinical impact and has improved the lives of patients. Who Can Apply? Applications are welcomed from researchers worldwide regardless of nationality or institutional affiliation. Eligible candidates include researchers working at: Universities Cancer research institutes Medical schools Hospitals Public research organizations Private research institutions International research centers Applicants are expected to demonstrate exceptional scientific excellence and a research record that has significantly advanced oncology. Selection Process Applications will be evaluated by an independent international jury composed of leading experts in oncology and biomedical research. Selection will be based exclusively on: Scientific excellence Originality of discoveries Clinical impact Innovation Long-term contribution to oncology Demonstrated benefits for patients Importantly, evaluations are conducted without regard to discipline, institution, or nationality, ensuring a fair and merit-based selection process. Why This Prize Matters Receiving the Gustave Roussy Prize represents more than financial recognition. It places recipients among the world s leading oncology researchers and highlights discoveries that have changed the landscape of cancer care. The prize also encourages continued innovation by providing dedicated funding for future research projects, ensuring that today s breakthroughs continue generating tomorrow s solutions. With cancer affecting millions globally, supporting transformative research remains essential for improving survival rates, enhancing quality of life, and developing more effective treatments. This award reinforces the importance of scientific collaboration and recognizes researchers whose work creates lasting benefits for humanity. Previous Laureates The prize has already honored internationally renowned scientists. Previous recipients include: 2025: Professor Charles Swanton 2026: Professor Luis Diaz Jr. These researchers were recognized for discoveries that have significantly influenced modern oncology and patient treatment. Award Ceremony The selected laureate for the 2027 edition will receive the prize on February 4, 2027 , during World Cancer Day , highlighting the global importance of cancer research and international collaboration in combating the disease. How to Apply Researchers interested in the Gustave Roussy Prize 2027 should complete the official online application before the deadline. Applicants are encouraged to present their most impactful scientific achievements, clearly demonstrate clinical relevance, and provide evidence showing how their discoveries have advanced cancer diagnosis, treatment, prevention, or patient care. Application Deadline: August 26, 2026, at 4:00 p.m. CEST. Interested applicants can find more information through the official Gustave Roussy website Discover more global awards opportunities on OFY here The post Gustave Roussy Prize 2027: €200,000 International Cancer Research Award Now Accepting Applications Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Aspen Institute Michael Cromartie Forum 2026 for Emerging Journalists in the USA: Apply for a Transformative Journalism Experience
summary: The Aspen Institute s Religion Society Program is now accepting applications for the Michael Cromartie Forum 2026 , an exclusive professional development opportunity designed for emerging journalists who want to deepen their understanding of the role religion plays in shaping politics, culture, public policy, and civic life. Taking place in Middleburg, Virginia, USA , from August 30 to September 1, 2026 , the Forum provides an intellectually engaging environment where early-career journalists can interact with experienced reporters, editors, scholars, and thought leaders through meaningful conversations rooted in the Aspen Institute s long-standing tradition of respectful dialogue and critical thinking. The Michael Cromartie Forum is more than a journalism conference—it is an immersive learning experience that equips participants with new perspectives, stronger reporting skills, and valuable professional networks that can influence their careers for years to come. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis and are expected to close around July 1, 2026. Early applications are strongly encouraged. About the Michael Cromartie Forum In today s rapidly evolving media landscape, journalists are expected to cover increasingly complex issues that intersect with politics, religion, identity, ethics, democracy, and culture. Religion continues to influence elections, policymaking, international affairs, education, social movements, and community life, making religious literacy an essential skill for modern journalism. Recognizing this need, the Aspen Institute s Religion Society Program established the Michael Cromartie Forum to help emerging journalists better understand how faith and religion influence public life while strengthening their ability to report accurately, fairly, and thoughtfully on these issues. Named in honor of the late Michael Cromartie , a respected advocate for improving religion reporting in mainstream media, the Forum continues his legacy by creating space for journalists to engage with scholars and experienced practitioners in open, nonpartisan dialogue. The program emphasizes curiosity, intellectual humility, and rigorous discussion rather than debate or advocacy. What Participants Can Expect During the three-day Forum, participants will engage in a series of carefully curated discussions, presentations, and interactive sessions covering topics at the intersection of religion and public life. The program brings together: Experienced journalists from leading media organizations. Editors with expertise in religion, politics, and public affairs reporting. Distinguished scholars and researchers. Public intellectuals and subject-matter experts. Fellow emerging journalists from diverse backgrounds. Rather than focusing on breaking news, the Forum encourages participants to examine the deeper historical, cultural, philosophical, and ethical dimensions of current events. This format allows journalists to gain insights that strengthen their long-term reporting and storytelling abilities. Why You Should Apply Participating in the Michael Cromartie Forum offers numerous professional and personal benefits, including: Exposure to leading scholars and experienced journalists. A deeper understanding of religion s influence on politics, society, and culture. Opportunities to improve reporting on complex public issues. Networking with fellow journalists from different media organizations. Participation in thoughtful, nonpartisan discussions. Membership in a growing community of journalism professionals committed to intellectual excellence. Professional growth through meaningful dialogue and collaborative learning. The Forum provides participants with the time and space to think critically about journalism beyond the pressures of daily newsroom deadlines. Who Is the Forum Designed For? The program is primarily intended for emerging journalists between the ages of 22 and 30 , although the organizers clearly state that there are no strict age requirements . Applicants may include: Print journalists. Broadcast journalists. Digital media reporters. Multimedia journalists. Editors early in their careers. Independent journalists. Freelance reporters. Journalism graduates beginning their professional careers. The organizers welcome applicants who demonstrate intellectual curiosity, a commitment to high-quality journalism, and an interest in understanding the role religion plays in contemporary society. Venue and Dates The 2026 Michael Cromartie Forum will take place in: Location: Middleburg, Virginia, USA Dates: August 30 – September 1, 2026 The setting is intentionally designed to encourage focused learning, reflection, relationship-building, and open dialogue among participants. Why Religion Reporting Matters Religion remains one of the most influential forces shaping communities across the world. It affects public policy, elections, education, healthcare, international relations, social justice movements, humanitarian work, and cultural identity. Despite this influence, religion is often misunderstood or underreported in mainstream journalism. The Michael Cromartie Forum seeks to bridge this gap by helping journalists: Improve religious literacy. Better understand diverse faith traditions. Report accurately on religion-related issues. Explore how religious beliefs intersect with politics and public life. Produce more nuanced, balanced, and informed journalism. These skills are increasingly valuable in today s interconnected and multicultural world. Professional Networking Opportunities One of the Forum s greatest strengths is the opportunity to build lasting professional relationships. Participants become part of a network that includes: National and international journalists. Editors. Academic experts. Public policy specialists. Religion scholars. Media leaders. These connections often lead to future collaborations, mentorship opportunities, and career advancement. How to Apply Interested applicants should complete the official application through the Aspen Institute Online Form. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis , meaning candidates may receive consideration as applications are submitted. Since spaces are limited, applying early significantly improves the chances of being considered. Expected Application Deadline: Around July 1, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to prepare a thoughtful application that demonstrates their journalism experience, curiosity about public affairs, and commitment to producing accurate, insightful reporting. Discover more global conference opportunities on OFY here The post Aspen Institute Michael Cromartie Forum 2026 for Emerging Journalists in the USA: Apply for a Transformative Journalism Experience appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: EU-LAC Women’s Political Leadership School 2026: Birregional Training for Women Leaders in Local Politics Across Latin America, the Caribbean, and the European Union
summary: The EU-LAC Foundation has officially launched the EU-LAC Birregional School of Local Political Leadership for Women 2026 , a high-impact leadership development programme designed to strengthen women’s political participation, advocacy skills, and leadership capacity at the local level across Latin America, the Caribbean, and the European Union . This initiative is a strategic space for training, exchange, and networking , bringing together women leaders from different political, community, and social backgrounds. The programme is built on a feminist, intersectional, and human rights-based approach , aiming to empower women to lead democratic, inclusive, and transformative change within their communities. The School combines virtual preparatory sessions with a face-to-face intensive training in Panama , ensuring both accessibility and meaningful intercultural exchange among participants. Objective of the EU-LAC Women’s Political Leadership School The main goal of the programme is to strengthen the capacity of women engaged in territorial and local leadership by providing: Advanced training in political leadership and governance Practical tools for communication, negotiation, and public management Opportunities for intergenerational mentoring Strong birregional networking between Europe, Latin America, and the Caribbean A platform for sharing lived experiences and community-based leadership strategies Through this initiative, the EU-LAC Foundation aims to reinforce women’s leadership in democratic governance and civic participation at the local level . Format of the Leadership School The programme follows a blended learning structure , combining digital and in-person components: Virtual Preparatory Phase Online training modules before the in-person session Introduction to leadership concepts, tools, and frameworks Networking and group discussions across regions In-Person Intensive Session Held in Panama (October 2026) High-level training workshops Interactive leadership sessions Cross-regional experience sharing Networking with women leaders from EU-LAC regions Follow-Up Activities Continued engagement through networks Participation in the EU-LAC WIN Network Knowledge sharing and leadership development continuation Profile of Applicants The programme is designed for two main categories of women leaders: Young Women Leaders Applicants should demonstrate: Membership in a network, organisation, programme, or institution Interest in territorial political leadership (formal or informal) Engagement in civic participation, feminist groups, or community initiatives Motivation to strengthen leadership skills and collaborate with women across regions Interest in EU-LAC relations, gender equality, or local development Willingness to join or engage with the EU-LAC WIN Network Senior Women Leaders Applicants should demonstrate: Strong and consolidated leadership experience in political or community spaces Proven impact in local governance, municipalities, councils, or civil society initiatives Membership in a recognised institution, network, or organisation Capacity to mentor and support younger women leaders Commitment to feminist and intersectional leadership principles Active contribution to community transformation and policy influence Required Application Documents All applications must be submitted in English or Spanish and include: Signed application form Proof of membership in a network, organisation, or institution Letter of endorsement or support from a relevant institution or organisation Submission Format PDF or MS Word format only Incomplete or late applications will not be considered Key Benefits of the Programme Selected participants will gain access to a wide range of professional and personal development opportunities, including: High-level leadership training in political and civic engagement Strengthening negotiation, communication, and governance skills Participation in a birregional leadership network Exposure to feminist and intersectional leadership frameworks Opportunities for mentorship and intergenerational learning Exchange of experiences across Europe, Latin America, and the Caribbean Capacity building in local governance and advocacy Financial Support (Scholarships) For selected participants who cannot self-finance their participation, the EU-LAC Foundation will provide: International travel coverage Hotel accommodation Local transport in Panama Daily stipend for meals and subsistence during the in-person phase Application Deadline Friday, 24 July 2026 (23:59 CEST – Germany time) Late or incomplete applications will not be accepted. How to Apply Applications must be submitted by email to: Email: eulacwin@eulacfoundation.org Attention: Dr Anna Barrera Subject Line: EU-LAC Women’s Political Leadership School 2026 + Applicant’s Name Selection Process Communication Applications will be reviewed by an internal selection committee Evaluation period: approximately 3 weeks after the deadline Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted via email Applicants are advised not to send repeated inquiries during the review period All official updates will be communicated electronically Final Overview The EU-LAC Women’s Political Leadership School 2026 is a unique opportunity for women leaders to build transformative leadership skills, strengthen democratic participation, and join a powerful international network committed to gender equality and social change. By combining training, mentorship, and intercultural dialogue, the programme empowers women to become stronger voices in local governance and community transformation. Applications should be sent to the attention of Dr Anna Barrera (EU-LAC Foundation). Email: eulacwin@eulacfoundation.org Subject line: EU-LAC Women’s Political Leadership School 2026 + Applicant’s Name Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global short courses opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post EU-LAC Women’s Political Leadership School 2026: Birregional Training for Women Leaders in Local Politics Across Latin America, the Caribbean, and the European Union appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Postdoctoral Research Fellowship (PDRF) Opportunities at the University of Johannesburg: Global Excellence and Societal Impact (GES 4.0) Initiative
summary: The University of Johannesburg (UJ) , through its Global Excellence and Stature (GES 4.0) Social Impact Initiative , is inviting applications from highly qualified and early-career researchers for its Postdoctoral Research Fellowship (PDRF) programme . This prestigious fellowship is designed to attract outstanding postdoctoral researchers who can contribute meaningfully to high-impact research, innovation, interdisciplinarity, and societal transformation. This fellowship is part of UJ’s long-term strategic vision (UJ Strategic Plan 2035), which positions the university as a globally competitive research institution addressing critical challenges facing society, the environment, governance systems, and technological transformation. The programme seeks exceptional scholars who demonstrate strong academic excellence, innovative thinking, and a commitment to solving real-world problems aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Overview of the Postdoctoral Research Fellowship Programme The PDRF at the University of Johannesburg is a full-time research fellowship that supports emerging scholars in conducting advanced research across priority thematic areas. Fellows will be embedded within recognized research entities, centres, institutes, SARChI Chairs, or strategic research programmes at UJ. This ensures that successful applicants are fully integrated into a vibrant academic environment that promotes collaboration, mentorship, and interdisciplinary research output. Priority Research Areas Applicants must align their research proposals with one or more of the following thematic areas: Sustainability, Climate Change, and Environmental Governance (ESG) Water, Energy, Food, and Environmental Security Public Health and Mental Health Systems Education and Child Development Migration, Social Justice, and Inequality Governance, Democracy, and Public Policy African Futures and Pan-African Thought Advanced Manufacturing and Industry 4.0 Smart Systems and Data Science Innovation, Entrepreneurship, and Societal Impact Artificial Intelligence and Digital Transformation These areas reflect UJ’s commitment to addressing global and African developmental challenges through cutting-edge research. Fellowship Conditions and Benefits Successful applicants will receive a highly competitive research support package designed to enable impactful academic work. Key Benefits Include: Competitive tax-free stipend of R250,000 per year Initial appointment for 12 months , renewable based on performance and funding availability Access to world-class UJ research infrastructure Integration into national and international research networks Mentorship from senior academics and research leaders Opportunities for professional development and academic growth The fellowship does not include relocation or accommodation support. Expectations of Postdoctoral Fellows Fellows are expected to contribute actively to the academic and research environment at UJ. Responsibilities include: Participation in capacity-building and research activities Co-supervision of postgraduate students Development of research funding proposals Presentation of research findings at conferences, seminars, and webinars Publishing research in peer-reviewed journals, books, or scholarly platforms Active participation in collaborative research networks Contribution to additional research initiatives aligned with host entities This ensures fellows not only conduct research but also contribute to the broader academic ecosystem. Eligibility and Minimum Requirements Applicants must meet the following criteria: Academic Requirements Hold a PhD or equivalent doctoral qualification (NQF Level 10) PhD must have been obtained within the last five years Must not have held a previous postdoctoral fellowship at the host discipline (preferred condition) Research Competencies Strong scholarly and research performance record Excellent academic writing and communication skills Proven research output or publications Other Requirements Ability to work full-time for the duration of the fellowship Demonstrated interest in interdisciplinary research and societal impact Preference for candidates with international collaboration experience Application Requirements Applicants must submit a complete application package including: Detailed Curriculum Vitae (CV) Certified copies of academic qualifications Copy of doctoral thesis abstract Motivation letter outlining suitability for the fellowship Research proposal (3–5 pages) aligned to a host research area Two academic reference letters Evidence of publications or research outputs Application Process Applications must be submitted electronically to the designated application platform or contact person provided by the university. For enquiries: Contact: Mr Thabiso Masango Email: thabisom@uj.ac.za Late or incomplete applications will not be considered. Value of the Fellowship The PDRF at UJ is not only a financial opportunity but also a career-defining academic experience. It provides: High-level research exposure in a globally recognized institution Access to interdisciplinary academic networks Opportunities to shape policy-relevant and impactful research Strong foundation for future academic or research leadership roles Application Deadline 15 July 2026 Conclusion The University of Johannesburg Postdoctoral Research Fellowship under the GES 4.0 Initiative represents one of the most competitive research opportunities in Africa. It is designed for emerging scholars who are ready to contribute to global knowledge production while addressing pressing societal challenges. Applicants with strong academic backgrounds, innovative research ideas, and interdisciplinary interests are strongly encouraged to apply. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Postdoctoral Research Fellowship (PDRF) Opportunities at the University of Johannesburg: Global Excellence and Societal Impact (GES 4.0) Initiative appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: African Road Safety Reporting Forum 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for African Journalists, Editors, and Media Influencers to Attend a Three-Day Forum in Abuja, Nigeria
summary: The Centre for Journalism Innovation and Development (CJID), through its Health Reporting Project, has officially opened applications for the African Road Safety Reporting Forum 2026 , a fully funded three-day in-person forum that will bring together journalists, editors, and media influencers from across Africa to strengthen road safety journalism and improve how road traffic crashes are reported across the continent. This unique forum seeks to transform road safety reporting by equipping media professionals with practical skills, technical knowledge, and collaborative opportunities to produce impactful stories that focus on accountability, prevention, and evidence-based solutions rather than simply reporting road crashes as isolated incidents. The forum will be held in Abuja, Nigeria , in September 2026 , bringing together 15 carefully selected media professionals who will work alongside experts from the World Health Organization (WHO), leading African editors, and experienced journalists to develop industry-standard editorial guidelines for road safety reporting across Africa. If you are passionate about public health reporting, investigative journalism, development journalism, or road safety advocacy, this is an excellent opportunity to expand your professional skills while contributing to safer roads across Africa. About the African Road Safety Reporting Forum Road traffic crashes remain one of the world s most pressing public health challenges. Globally, road crashes are the leading cause of death among children and young people between the ages of 5 and 29 years . Even more concerning, road traffic deaths continue to rise more rapidly in Africa than in any other region of the world. Despite the enormous impact of road crashes, media reporting often fails to reflect the true scale of the crisis. Many news reports portray crashes as unfortunate accidents that simply happen, rather than highlighting the preventable systemic issues behind them, including: Poor road infrastructure Weak enforcement of road safety laws Policy failures Inadequate governance Unsafe transport systems Lack of accountability In addition, media coverage frequently focuses on blaming individual road users while overlooking broader structural causes that contribute to road traffic injuries and fatalities. The African Road Safety Reporting Forum aims to change this narrative by helping journalists produce stories that encourage policy reforms, accountability, and sustainable road safety solutions. Objectives of the Forum Participants will work together to: Develop industry-standard editorial guidelines for road safety reporting in Africa. Improve the quality and impact of road safety journalism. Produce compelling road safety stories using newly developed reporting approaches. Promote solutions-focused journalism. Encourage accountability-driven reporting. Strengthen collaboration among African media professionals working on public health and transport issues. Build a continent-wide network of journalists committed to improving road safety coverage. Who Should Apply? The forum is designed for professionals working within Africa s media landscape who are interested in improving public health and road safety reporting. Applicants should be: Journalists Editors Media influencers Applicants must also demonstrate a strong interest in: Public health journalism Road safety reporting Accountability journalism Development reporting Investigative reporting Although the forum particularly seeks participants from Kenya, Nigeria, Uganda, Ethiopia, and South Africa , applications from other African countries are also welcomed. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet all of the following requirements: Be based in Kenya, Nigeria, Uganda, Ethiopia, South Africa, or another African country. Be an editor, journalist, or media influencer. Demonstrate an interest in public health or road safety reporting. Be available to attend the three-day physical forum in September 2026. Be willing to develop and publish a road safety story following the forum. Be willing to participate in an online working group that will continue developing African road safety reporting guidelines. Benefits of the African Road Safety Reporting Forum Successful applicants will receive several valuable benefits, including: 1. Practical Professional Training Participants will receive hands-on training in: Solutions-focused journalism Accountability reporting Effective road safety reporting Public health communication 2. Access to Leading Experts Participants will interact directly with: WHO technical experts Leading African editors Experienced journalists Road safety professionals 3. Influence Future Journalism Standards Participants will contribute to creating editorial guidelines that may influence road safety reporting across Africa. 4. Produce High-Impact Stories Participants will develop and publish road safety stories that reflect the newly developed editorial standards. 5. Fully Funded Participation Selected participants will receive: Full travel support Accommodation Daily stipend throughout the forum Why This Forum Matters Media plays a powerful role in shaping public understanding and influencing policy decisions. By improving how road traffic crashes are reported, journalists can help: Raise public awareness Encourage stronger government accountability Promote evidence-based road safety policies Influence positive behavioural change Reduce preventable road deaths across Africa This forum therefore represents not only a professional development opportunity but also a chance to contribute to meaningful public health improvements across the continent. Application Deadline Deadline: 10 July 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted, and successful applicants will receive notification via email. Final Thoughts The African Road Safety Reporting Forum 2026 offers an outstanding opportunity for African journalists, editors, and media influencers to improve their reporting skills while contributing to a safer future for millions of road users across Africa. Through expert-led training, collaboration with leading media professionals, and the development of continent-wide editorial standards, participants will play a significant role in transforming road safety journalism from reactive reporting into impactful storytelling that promotes accountability and lasting policy change. Interested applicants should prepare their submissions early and ensure they meet all eligibility requirements before the application deadline of 10 July 2026 . Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global short courses opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post African Road Safety Reporting Forum 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for African Journalists, Editors, and Media Influencers to Attend a Three-Day Forum in Abuja, Nigeria appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Ireland Fellows Programme 2027/28: The Complete Guide to Ireland’s Fully Funded Government Scholarship for Africa, Asia, Latin America, Palestine, and Small Island Developing States
summary: The Government of Ireland has officially opened applications for the Ireland Fellows Programme 2027/28 one of the most prestigious, fully funded, and globally reaching scholarship programmes offered by any government in the world. Administered by the Department of Foreign Affairs of Ireland and delivered in partnership with Irish higher education institutions, the Ireland Fellows Programme provides exceptional professionals from developing countries and Small Island Developing States across Africa, Asia, Latin America, the Caribbean, and the Pacific with the opportunity to pursue fully funded master s degree programmes at leading universities in Ireland. Applications for the 2027/28 academic year are open from Monday, 29 June to Sunday, 26 July 2026 . This is a narrow and firm application window, and prospective candidates are strongly encouraged to prepare their applications well in advance. This comprehensive guide brings together all available information across every strand of the Ireland Fellows Programme Africa, Asia, Latin America, SIDS, and Palestine in one place, providing everything eligible candidates need to know about the programme s aims, funding, eligibility criteria, application process, and what to expect at each stage. What Is the Ireland Fellows Programme? The Ireland Fellows Programme is the Government of Ireland s flagship international scholarship initiative, funded through Irish Aid, Ireland s official overseas development assistance programme. It is designed to support emerging leaders from developing countries and Small Island Developing States by providing access to world-class postgraduate education in Ireland. The programme has three overarching aims: To nurture future leaders in eligible countries by equipping them with advanced academic knowledge and professional skills To develop in-country capacity to achieve national Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and contribute to long-term development outcomes in the recipients home countries To build positive, lasting relationships between Ireland and partner countries and regions across the globe The Ireland Fellows Programme is fully funded, meaning that selected candidates receive comprehensive financial support for the entirety of their studies in Ireland. It is one of the few government scholarships in the world that covers not only tuition fees but also living costs, travel, accommodation, insurance, and visa fees. What Does the Ireland Fellows Programme Cover? The Ireland Fellows Programme award is fully funded. For most strands, the award includes: Full payment of academic tuition fees at an Irish higher education institution Economy-class return flights from the recipient s country of residence to Ireland Visa application fees Medical and travel insurance for the duration of the programme The Irish residence permit fee A monthly living stipend (for the Palestine strand, this is 700 euros per month, subject to review) A settling-in allowance to cover items needed upon arrival in Ireland A completion and departure allowance at the end of the study period Student accommodation arranged and paid for during Trimesters 1 and 2 (typically September to May); and for Trimester 3, a monthly accommodation stipend of between 800 and 1,000 euros depending on location in Ireland (Palestine strand figures, subject to review) It is important to note that the fellowship covers the recipient only. Financial support and visa assistance for spouses or dependents are not included within the scope of the programme under any strand. The Strands of the Ireland Fellows Programme The Ireland Fellows Programme is organized into several distinct geographic strands, each with its own eligible countries, specific named awards, and in some cases unique eligibility conditions. Below is a complete breakdown of every strand. Strand 1: Ireland Fellows Programme Africa The Africa strand is the largest and most geographically diverse strand of the Ireland Fellows Programme, covering 24 countries across the continent. Eligible Countries (Africa Strand) Angola, Burundi, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Djibouti, Eritrea, Eswatini, Ethiopia, Ghana, Kenya, Lesotho, Liberia, Malawi, Mozambique, Nigeria, Rwanda, Senegal, Sierra Leone, Somalia, South Sudan, Sudan, Tanzania, Uganda, Zambia, Zimbabwe. Named Fellowships Within the Africa Strand Several countries within the Africa strand have dedicated, named fellowship awards with specific additional guidance: Kader Asmal Fellowship South Africa Roger Casement Fellowship Nigeria Sean Mac Bride Fellowship Namibia Archbishop Michael Courtney Fellowship Burundi Frank Ferguson Fellowship in Engineering Zambia SIDS-Africa Fellowship Cabo Verde, Comoros, Guinea-Bissau, Mauritius, Seychelles, Sao Tome and Principe (see SIDS strand below) Candidates from Burundi, Nigeria, Namibia, South Africa, and Zambia are advised to review both the general Africa strand guidance and the specific named fellowship page for their country before applying. Eligible Courses (Africa Strand) Programmes span a wide range of subject areas including agriculture, health, education, human rights, computer science, engineering, business, and more. All eligible programmes are listed in the Directory of Eligible Programmes published by the Ireland Fellows Programme. Eligibility Requirements (Africa Strand) To be eligible for the Africa strand commencing in the academic year 2027, applicants must: Be a resident national of one of the eligible African countries listed above Have a minimum of two or three years of substantial work experience directly relevant to the proposed programme of study (the exact requirement varies by country applicants should refer to page 8 of the guidance note for country-specific details) Hold a bachelor s degree from an accredited, government-recognized higher education institution with a minimum GPA of 3.0 on a 4.0 scale, awarded in 2015 or later (i.e. within the last 12 years). A second-class honour Grade 2 may be accepted if the applicant has substantial and directly relevant work experience Not already hold a qualification at master s level or higher, and not be currently undertaking or due to start a master s programme in the 2026/27 academic year (note: candidates from Senegal and Liberia should contact their respective Irish Embassy about this criterion) Be applying to commence a new master s level programme in Ireland no sooner than August 2027 Demonstrate leadership abilities and aspirations, a commitment to achieving the SDGs within their country, and a commitment to building positive relationships with Ireland Have identified and selected three programmes from the Directory of Eligible Programmes that are relevant to their academic and professional background Have a clear understanding of the academic and English language requirements for all chosen programmes Not have applied to the Ireland Fellows Programme more than once previously Be in a position to take up the Fellowship in the 2027/28 academic year Important Note: Invitation-Only Countries (Africa Strand) In the following countries, applications are by invitation only: Burundi, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Eswatini, Eritrea, Kenya, Liberia, Malawi, Sierra Leone, Somalia, Sudan, and Zambia. In these countries, fellowships are restricted to staff of government departments and NGOs working in partnership with Irish Aid, and to personnel of organizations aligned with Irish Aid s development strategy that have already been identified by the Embassy of Ireland. Applications from outside these established partnerships will not be eligible. Prospective applicants from these countries should contact the relevant Embassy of Ireland or their employer to determine whether they are eligible. Learn More Here Strand 2: Ireland Fellows Programme Asia The Asia strand covers three countries in Southeast Asia and provides opportunities for professionals from Vietnam, Cambodia, and Lao PDR to pursue master s level education in Ireland. Eligible Countries (Asia Strand) Vietnam, Cambodia, and Lao PDR. Eligibility Requirements (Asia Strand) To be eligible for the Asia strand commencing in the academic year 2027, applicants must: Be a resident national of Vietnam, Cambodia, or Lao PDR Have a minimum of two years of relevant work experience (Vietnam) or three years of relevant work experience (Cambodia and Lao PDR), directly relevant to the proposed programme of study Hold a bachelor s degree from an accredited, government-recognized institution with a minimum GPA of 3.0 on a 4.0 scale, awarded in 2015 or later Not already hold a qualification at master s level or higher, and not be enrolled in or starting a master s programme in 2026/27 Be applying to commence a new master s level programme in Ireland no sooner than August 2027 Demonstrate leadership abilities, SDG commitment, and commitment to building positive relationships with Ireland Have identified and selected three programmes from the Directory of Eligible Programmes Have a clear understanding of the academic and English language requirements for all chosen programmes Not have applied to the Ireland Fellows Programme more than once previously Be in a position to take up the Fellowship in 2027/28 Learn More Here Strand 3: Ireland Fellows Programme Latin America The Latin America strand covers countries across Central and South America and is organized into three named awards, each focused on a different thematic area. Eligible Countries and Named Awards (Latin America Strand) Central America Award: Belize, Costa Rica, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras Roger Casement Award in Human Rights: Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, Venezuela Cecilia Grierson Award in Equality: Argentina, Bolivia, Paraguay Note: Belize is classified as a Small Island Developing State (SIDS) but candidates from Belize must apply via the Central America Award and may not apply via the SIDS Fellowship strand. Eligibility Requirements (Latin America Strand) To be eligible for the Latin America strand commencing in the academic year 2027, applicants must: Be a resident national of one of the eligible Latin American countries listed above Have a minimum of three years of substantial work experience directly relevant to the proposed programme of study Hold a bachelor s degree from an accredited, government-recognized institution with a minimum GPA of 3.0 on a 4.0 scale, awarded in 2015 or later Not already hold a qualification at master s level or higher, and not be enrolled in or due to start a master s programme in 2026/27 Be applying to commence a new master s level programme in Ireland no sooner than August 2027 Demonstrate leadership abilities, SDG commitment, and commitment to building positive relationships with Ireland Have identified and selected three programmes from the Directory of Eligible Programmes Have a clear understanding of the academic and English language requirements for all chosen programmes Not have applied to the Ireland Fellows Programme more than once previously Be in a position to take up the Fellowship in 2027/28 Learn More Here Strand 4: Ireland Fellows Programme Small Island Developing States (SIDS) The SIDS strand of the Ireland Fellows Programme is dedicated to nationals and residents of Small Island Developing States across four geographic regions: Africa and the Indian Ocean, Asia, the Caribbean, and the Pacific. Eligible Countries by Region (SIDS Strand) Africa, Indian Ocean, and South China Sea: Cabo Verde, Comoros, Guinea-Bissau, Mauritius, Sao Tome and Principe, Seychelles Asia: Maldives, Timor-Leste Caribbean: Antigua and Barbuda, Bahamas, Barbados, Cuba, Dominica, Dominican Republic, Grenada, Guyana, Haiti, Jamaica, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Lucia, St Vincent and the Grenadines, Suriname, Trinidad and Tobago Pacific: Cook Islands, Fiji, Kiribati, Marshall Islands, Micronesia (Federated States of), Nauru, Niue, Palau, Papua New Guinea, Samoa, Solomon Islands, Tonga, Tuvalu, Vanuatu An important note on residency: applicants must be a national of one of the eligible SIDS countries and also be resident in one of the eligible SIDS countries. They may be a national of one country and resident in another, provided both are within the same region as defined by the programme. Eligibility Requirements (SIDS Strand) To be eligible for the SIDS strand commencing in the academic year 2027, applicants must: Be a national of an eligible SIDS country and resident in an eligible SIDS country (within the same region) Have a minimum of three years of substantial work experience directly relevant to the proposed programme of study Hold a bachelor s degree from an accredited, government-recognized institution with a minimum GPA of 3.0 on a 4.0 scale, awarded in 2015 or later Not already hold a qualification at master s level or higher, and not be enrolled in or due to start a master s programme in 2026/27 Be applying to commence a new master s level programme in Ireland no sooner than August 2027 Demonstrate leadership abilities, SDG commitment, and commitment to building positive relationships with Ireland Have identified and selected three programmes from the Directory of Eligible Programmes Have a clear understanding of the academic and English language requirements for all chosen programmes Not have applied to the Ireland Fellows Programme more than once previously Be in a position to take up the Fellowship in 2027/28 Learn More Here Strand 5: Ireland-Palestine Scholarship Programme (IPSP) The Ireland-Palestine Scholarship Programme is a distinct and separately administered strand of the broader Ireland Fellows global scholarship programme, run by the Representative Office of Ireland in Ramallah in partnership with Amideast. It is specifically for Palestinians resident in the occupied Palestinian territory. Please note: applications for the 2026/2027 academic year under the IPSP are now closed. The programme typically opens annually in September. The information below reflects the most recently published programme details. What the IPSP Covers The IPSP offers 30 full scholarships for one-year master s level programmes at 19 higher education institutions across Ireland. The award is fully funded and includes: Full academic tuition fees Visa application fees Medical and travel insurance Economy-class return flights to and from Ireland Irish residence permit fee A monthly living stipend of 700 euros (subject to review) A settling-in allowance upon arrival in Ireland A completion and departure allowance at the end of the study period Student accommodation arranged and paid for during Trimesters 1 and 2 (typically September to May) A monthly accommodation stipend of 800 to 1,000 euros during Trimester 3, depending on location in Ireland (subject to review) Eligibility Requirements (IPSP) To be eligible for the Ireland-Palestine Scholarship Programme, candidates must: Be a Palestinian currently resident in the occupied Palestinian territory, holding a Palestinian or Jerusalem ID Have identified and selected three relevant courses from the Directory of Eligible Postgraduate Courses Hold a bachelor s degree from an accredited institution with a minimum GPA of 80 percent or 3.0 (or equivalent standard) Have achieved the necessary academic standard to be accepted onto a master s degree course at a higher education institution in Ireland Be applying to commence a new qualification not seeking funding for a course already commenced or beginning before scholarship awards are notified Not already hold a qualification at master s level or higher Be able to take up the scholarship in the relevant academic year Submit a valid English language proficiency score report dated September 2024 or later, with a minimum score of: 6.5 on IELTS, or 550 on the Institutional Testing Program (ITP), or 80 on Internet-based TOEFL (iBT), or 120 overall on Duolingo (with at least 105 in each sub-score) Importantly, prior professional working experience is not required for the IPSP. The programme is also committed to providing equal opportunity to all applicants, irrespective of sex, religious beliefs, race and ethnicity, age, disability status, gender identity and expression, geographic location, income, socio-economic status, or any other dimension of diversity. Learn More Here The Application Process: All Strands (Africa, Asia, Latin America, SIDS) For all strands except Palestine (which has its own separate process), the application process for the Ireland Fellows Programme consists of three stages: Stage 1: Preliminary Application The initial stage requires applicants to complete and submit an online preliminary application. This stage is used to screen candidates for basic eligibility and to identify the most promising applicants for progression to the detailed application stage. Stage 2: Detailed Application Shortlisted candidates from Stage 1 will be invited to submit a more comprehensive detailed application, providing additional information about their background, professional experience, leadership record, SDG commitment, and specific programme choices. Stage 3: Interviews Candidates who progress through Stage 2 will be invited to participate in an interview as the final selection step. Successful candidates will be notified following the conclusion of the interview process. Application Deadline Applications for the 2027/28 academic year are open from Monday, 29 June 2026 to Sunday, 26 July 2026. This is a strict and firm deadline. Late applications will not be considered. Prospective applicants are strongly encouraged to gather all required documents and begin preparing their applications immediately. English Language Requirements All Irish higher education institutions require a high standard of English language proficiency, and this requirement is non-negotiable with no exceptions for any applicant to any institution. For all strands, English language proficiency will be verified through testing English language certification is also required for Irish visa applications Shortlisted applicants who do not yet hold the required English language certification for their chosen master s programmes will be required to take the Duolingo English Test, which is accepted by most Irish higher education institutions If shortlisted or selected as a reserve candidate, and the chosen course or institution does not accept Duolingo, the applicant will be required to take an IELTS exam in January 2027 For the Palestine strand specifically, applicants must already submit a valid English language proficiency score with their initial application with a minimum score of 6.5 on IELTS, 550 on ITP, 80 on iBT TOEFL, or 120 on Duolingo (with at least 105 in each sub-score). This score must be dated September 2024 or later. Choosing Your Programmes: The Directory of Eligible Programmes All applicants must select three postgraduate programmes from the official Directory of Eligible Programmes published by the Ireland Fellows Programme. Eligible subject areas span a wide range of fields including: Agriculture and food science Health and medicine-related fields Education and teacher training Human rights and law Computer science and information technology Engineering and technology Business and management Environmental science and sustainability And many more Applicants are responsible for ensuring that they understand the specific academic and English language entry requirements for each of the three programmes they select, as these requirements vary by institution and course. Selecting programmes that align closely with an applicant s academic background, professional experience, and development goals will strengthen the overall application considerably. Key Tips for a Strong Application While the Ireland Fellows Programme does not publish a formal scoring rubric, the following principles derived from the stated aims and eligibility criteria of the programme are essential for building a competitive application: Ensure that all eligibility criteria are met in full before beginning the application Select three programmes from the Directory that genuinely align with your professional background and career aspirations Demonstrate clear, specific, and evidence-based leadership experience not just leadership potential Articulate a concrete and credible plan for how the master s degree will contribute to SDG achievement in your home country Show a genuine understanding of and commitment to building positive Ireland-country relationships Prepare your English language documentation well in advance Apply through the correct strand and the correct Embassy, especially for countries with named fellowships or invitation-only processes Contact the relevant Embassy of Ireland early if you have any questions about your eligibility For more information and to apply, visit HERE Conclusion The Ireland Fellows Programme 2027/28 is one of the most comprehensive, fully funded, and globally inclusive government scholarship programmes available to professionals from developing countries and Small Island Developing States. Spanning Africa, Asia, Latin America, the Caribbean, the Pacific, and Palestine, the programme offers emerging leaders an extraordinary opportunity to pursue a master s degree at a world-class Irish university with all costs covered, from flights and tuition to living expenses and insurance. With applications open only from 29 June to 26 July 2026, the window to apply is critically short. For professionals across the 70-plus eligible countries who meet the eligibility criteria holding a relevant bachelor s degree, possessing the required work experience, demonstrating leadership and SDG commitment, and not yet holding a master s qualification this programme represents a once-in-a-career opportunity to access international education, build a global network, and return home equipped to drive meaningful development in their communities and countries. Review the Directory of Eligible Programmes, contact your nearest Embassy of Ireland to confirm your eligibility and strand, gather your documents, and submit your application before the deadline of 26 July 2026. The Ireland Fellows Programme has transformed the careers and communities of hundreds of professionals around the world and the 2027/28 cohort could include you. Discover more global Scholarship and Fellowship opportunities on OFY. The post Ireland Fellows Programme 2027/28: The Complete Guide to Ireland s Fully Funded Government Scholarship for Africa, Asia, Latin America, Palestine, and Small Island Developing States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: IFC SME Growth Accelerator 2026: Up to USD 1.5 Million in Grant Funding for High-Potential SMEs in Emerging Markets
summary: High-potential small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) operating in emerging markets have an exciting opportunity to accelerate their business growth through the IFC SME Growth Accelerator 2026 . The programme offers grant funding of up to USD 1.5 million , alongside mentorship, business networking opportunities, and potential follow-on financing to help ambitious businesses expand their operations, enter new markets, and create quality employment. The SME Growth Accelerator is an initiative of the International Finance Corporation (IFC) , a member of the World Bank Group dedicated to supporting private sector development in emerging economies. The programme is designed to help promising SMEs overcome growth barriers by providing financial support and strategic business development resources. If your enterprise is looking to scale its impact, strengthen its competitive advantage, or expand into regional or international markets, this funding opportunity could provide the resources needed to achieve your growth ambitions. About the IFC SME Growth Accelerator Small and medium-sized enterprises are the backbone of many developing economies, contributing significantly to employment, innovation, and economic growth. However, many SMEs struggle to access the financing and technical support required to expand successfully. The IFC SME Growth Accelerator seeks to bridge this gap by supporting businesses with grant funding and complementary business development services. Beyond financial assistance, the programme aims to strengthen the long-term sustainability and competitiveness of participating enterprises.
